$description: Responsive design strategy: breakpoints, interaction modality, orientation, and tablet-first principles. The portal's layout adapts across four viewport tiers, three interaction modalities, and two orientations. Viewport width alone is an unreliable proxy — interaction capability, orientation, and connectivity all matter. Source: DES-049.

breakpoints.$description: Four named viewport tiers. These are the universal responsive vocabulary for all surfaces consuming the design language. Named tiers prevent magic numbers.

mobile.character: Single column, full-width, touch-first. Content fills the viewport.
mobile.reader-behavior: Single column, full-width text. Landscape: line length capped.

tablet.character: First-class reading surface. NOT an interpolation between mobile and desktop.
tablet.reader-behavior: Wider margins than mobile, narrower than desktop. Landscape: two-column opt-in.
tablet.design-stance: Tablets are arguably the ideal form factor for sustained book reading — larger than a phone, more portable than a laptop, usable in bed, in a garden, or at a study group. The portal treats tablet as a distinct experience tier.

desktop.character: Full layout. Side panels visible. Hover affordances available.
desktop.reader-behavior: Generous margins. Related Teachings side panel visible.

wide.character: Reader centered at max-content width. Extra space used for auxiliary content.
wide.reader-behavior: Reader centered, side panel always visible.

interaction-modality.$description: Viewport width does not reveal interaction capability. A tablet with a keyboard case behaves like a desktop; a touchscreen laptop behaves like a tablet. CSS media features for interaction-dependent behavior.

desktop-class.affordances: Hover tooltips, dwell icon reveal on hover, fine cursor interactions.

touch-primary.affordances: Long-press dwell, native share sheet, enlarged tap targets.
touch-primary.touch-target: 48px minimum for key navigation and form inputs

hybrid.affordances: Both hover and touch affordances available. Favor touch for primary actions.

component-implications.dwell-mode: Hover-icon trigger for desktop-class. Long-press for touch-primary. Both on hybrid.
component-implications.share-menu: navigator.share (native sheet) for touch-primary. Custom share menu for desktop-class.
component-implications.glossary-terms: Hover tooltip for desktop-class. Tap-to-reveal for touch-primary.

orientation.$description: The portal does not lock orientation. Layout adapts to portrait and landscape.

phone-landscape.treatment: Single column with wider margins. Line length capped to prevent excessively long lines.

tablet-landscape.treatment: Two-column option: chapter text left, Related Teachings/footnotes right. Opt-in via reader settings (localStorage). The orientation transition is the natural breakpoint, not viewport width.
tablet-landscape.default: Single column (not two-column by default — some seekers prefer single regardless)

presentation-landscape.treatment: Landscape preferred for group reading. Text fills viewport width with proportionally larger font.

tablet-as-first-class.$description: Specific tablet design decisions that prevent 'just interpolate' thinking.
tablet-as-first-class.reader-margins: 48px at 768px width, scaling to 64px at 1023px. Not cramped, not wasted.
tablet-as-first-class.related-teachings: Bottom sheet on portrait (matches mobile). Side panel on landscape (matches desktop). Orientation drives this, not viewport width.
tablet-as-first-class.two-column-reader: Opt-in setting (reader settings popover). Stored in localStorage. Never forced.
tablet-as-first-class.touch-targets: 44px minimum (not enlarged to 48px like feature-phone pages — tablet touch precision is closer to desktop)

low-bandwidth-suggestion.$description: When navigator.connection.effectiveType reports '2g' or 'slow-2g', display a one-time suggestion banner (not a modal). Never forced — always a suggestion. Source: DES-049, extending ADR-006 §1.
low-bandwidth-suggestion.copy: Your connection appears slow. Switch to text-only mode for a faster experience?
low-bandwidth-suggestion.persistence: Dismissed preference in sessionStorage (resets per session). Allows re-suggestion if conditions change.
low-bandwidth-suggestion.style: Warm cream palette, not alert style. This is ambient information per PRI-08.

future-considerations.foldable-devices: Monitor CSS env(fold-left) / env(fold-right) proposals. No action until market share warrants.
future-considerations.smart-displays: API-first architecture enables smart display integration without new breakpoints.
future-considerations.tv-casting: data-mode='cast' alongside data-mode='present' if demand emerges. 10-foot UI paradigm.
